page: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v5 is an EASv5-family memory-optimized virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 32 dedicated vCPUs derived from 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Memory performance is a notable strength, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, although uncached large-block read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks remain average. Image processing and small-model LLM text generation also demonstrate strong performance, while large-model LLM prompt processing is weak, reflecting the lack of hardware acceleration.

Given its high memory density of 16 GB per vCPU, the E64-32as_v5 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-speed local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, requiring reliance on external network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy operations. This server is suited for memory-intensive applications, database caching, enterprise resource planning systems, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v5 is an EASv5-series memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server features 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. It is configured with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside average multi-core and database execution capabilities. The absence of local storage represents a key tradeoff, requiring reliance on external Premium Storage. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads, including database caching, enterprise applications, and memory-bound data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v5 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM. It delivers strong single-core and memory performance for enterprise databases and caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32as_v5 is an EASv5 virtual machine with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM density and strong single-core performance for enterprise databases and memory-bound workloads.

Equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing 16.0 GB of memory per core
